common.title: MyAstroBoard
common.cache_not_ready: Data is being fetched. Please try again shortly.
common.comming_soon: Cette fonctionnalité est en cours de développement et sera bientôt disponible !

about.title: About MyAstroBoard
about.summary_text: MyAstroBoard is a self-hosted astronomy dashboard for observation and astrophotography planning.
about.sponsor_text: If MyAstroBoard helps you, you can support its maintenance and development.
about.funding_hint: See all active support links in FUNDING.yml.
about.license_text: MyAstroBoard is licensed under AGPL-3.0. Source code remains available for deployed versions.

astro_weather.description_horizon_graph: Horizon altitude profile at your location
astro_weather.no_observation_periods: No optimal observation periods found in the next 24 hours
astro_weather.footer_source: Data source: Open-Meteo weather inputs + 100% local MyAstroBoard astrophotography analysis.

best_window.info_1: Best next window is calculated within three differents modes, return is in hours:
best_window.info_2: Sun altitude < -18° AND Moon altitude < 0° => No moon on sky
best_window.info_3: Sun < -18° AND Moon altitude < 5° => Moon, but negligible
best_window.info_4: Sun < -18° AND Moon illumination < 15% => Visible moon, but faint
best_window.no_dark_window: No dark window found in the next 24 hours
best_window.failed_to_load_dark_window_data: Failed to load dark window data

moon.info_1: Dark time is calculated according to three different modes, the return is in hours:
moon.astrophotography_score_hint: Score based on type, visibility, altitude, and duration
moon.failed_to_load_lunar_eclipse_data: Failed to load lunar eclipse data
moon.footer_source_report: Data source: 100% local astronomical calculations (Moon ephemeris and derived events).
moon.footer_source_planner: Data source: 100% local MyAstroBoard calculations for next nights and astrophotography score.
moon.footer_source_best_window: Data source: 100% local MyAstroBoard dark-window and best-window calculations.

sun.astrophotography_score_hint: Score based on type, visibility, altitude, and duration
sun.failed_to_load_solar_eclipse_data: Failed to load solar eclipse data
sun.footer_source: Data source: 100% local astronomical calculations (sunrise, sunset, and twilight times).

description.very_low: Aurora possible only at very high latitudes
description.good: Aurora likely visible across northern regions
description.excellent: Aurora very likely, possibly at lower latitudes
description.severe_storm: Intense aurora activity, visible at lower latitudes

colors.red: Red (high altitude, >300 km, with strong activity)

aurora.alert_1: The Kp index values are provided by NOAA. Sudden changes between 'Now' and forecasted values may indicate predicted geomagnetic events or storms.
aurora.alert_2: These forecasts are subject to change and reflect NOAA's latest predictions.

tips.1: Travel away from light pollution (cities) for best viewing
tips.2: Look towards the northern horizon during the recommended window
tips.3: Dress warmly - aurora hunting can require waiting outside in cold
tips.4: Higher Kp index means aurora could be visible further south
tips.5: Green aurora is most common, red is from high altitude oxygen
tips.6: Better visibility on clear, moonless nights

aurora.failed_to_load_predictions: Failed to load aurora predictions. Please try again later.
aurora.footer_source: Data source: NOAA SWPC space weather feeds + local MyAstroBoard visibility scoring.

seeing_forecast.title: Seeing Forecast

seeing_descriptions.1: Excellent (< 0.5 arcsec) - Perfect for planetary imaging
seeing_descriptions.2: Good (0.5 - 1 arcsec) - Very good for planetary imaging
seeing_descriptions.3: Moderate (1 - 2 arcsec) - Fair for planetary imaging
seeing_descriptions.5: Very Poor (> 4 arcsec) - Unsuitable for planetary imaging

seeing_forecast.footer_source: Data source: 7Timer seeing forecast + local normalization and best-window scoring.
seeing_forecast.unavailable_no_usable_values: 7Timer's public numeric API returned undefined seeing values for this cycle. The website chart may still show icons, but no usable numeric seeing values are currently provided.
seeing_forecast.unavailable_fetch_failed: Seeing forecast is temporarily unavailable because the 7Timer request failed. Please try again later.

tips.1: Excellent seeing is rare - take advantage of it for high-resolution planetary imaging
tips.2: Seeing is affected by atmospheric turbulence, temperature gradients, and altitude
tips.3: Best seeing typically occurs during stable atmospheric conditions
tips.4: Jupiter and Saturn are best targets during good seeing (1-2 arcsec)
tips.5: The Moon can be imaged even with poor seeing due to its brightness

seeing_forecast.failed_to_load_forecast: Failed to load seeing forecast. Please try again later.
seeing_forecast.scale_mapping_info: 7Timer raw seeing values (1-8) are normalized to this app's 5-level quality scale (1-5) to keep labels and descriptions consistent.

iss.info_tab: ISS visible passages for the next {days} days (sunlit ISS and dark-enough sky), computed for your configured location and timezone.
iss.no_passes: No visible ISS passage found in the selected forecast window.
iss.celestrak_blocked_description: MyAstroBoard detected a Celestrak policy block for this host. Automatic Celestrak queries are paused and alternative providers are being used.
iss.celestrak_restart_hint: Open the check link from the same external IP as this MyAstroBoard host. After confirming the block is lifted, you can restart Celestrak crawling.
iss.celestrak_restart_confirm: Confirm restart: use this only after Celestrak has removed the block for this host IP. Continue?
iss.footer_source: Data source: Active TLE provider is {source}. Orbital pass geometry is calculated locally from this TLE.

spaceflight.data_source: Data and images provided by The Space Devs via Launch Library 2
spaceflight.english_only_notice: This content is only available in English.

calendar.no_significant_events: No significant astronomical events in the next 30 days.

settings.customize_info: Personalize your dashboard defaults and display preferences. These settings apply only to your account.
settings.security_password_change_info: Change your account password. Your current password is required for confirmation.
settings.location_info: Location parameters are used for accurate weather, astronomical calculations, and SkyTonight scheduling.
settings.astrodex_info_1: The Astrodex is shared by default between all users. Thats mean every body can fill the Astrodex, but only the owner of the picture can edit or delete it.
settings.astrodex_info_2: If needed, you can set up a private Astrodex for each user.
settings.astrodex_private_info: Enable this option to set up a private Astrodex for each user
settings.sky_quality_info: Bortle class describes your site's light pollution level. Find it at lightpollutionmap.info (World Atlas layer).
settings.sky_quality_sqm_info: Enter if you have a real SQM meter reading. Overrides the Bortle midpoint estimate.
settings.sky_quality_disabled_info: Leaving both empty disables light pollution integration.
settings.save_actions_info: When new SkyTonight settings are saved, a manual run may be required to see changes on dashboard, or wait for the next scheduled run.
settings.feature_horizon_info: Plot the horizon based on `horizon.anchor_points` to configure below
settings.feature_objects_info: Calculate and plot deep sky objects based on the chosen target list
settings.feature_bodies_info: Calculate and plot solar system bodies
settings.feature_alttime_info: Calculate and plot altitude vs time diagrams
settings.skytonight_info: These parameters are used by SkyTonight internals.
settings.altitude_min_title: Minimum altitude in degrees above horizon (typical: 30°)
settings.altitude_max_title: Maximum altitude in degrees above horizon (typical: 80°)
settings.size_min_info: Minimum object size in arc minutes
settings.size_max_info: Maximum object size in arc minutes
settings.moon_sep_info: Minimum separation from moon in degrees (overridden if 'Use Moon Illumination' is checked)
settings.time_threshold_info: Fraction of darkness time object must be observable (0.0-1.0, e.g., 0.5 = 50%)
settings.max_targets_info: Maximum number of targets to calculate (recommended: ~45 for MQTT, ~60 otherwise)
settings.use_moon_illumination_info: If enabled, minimum moon separation is derived from moon illumination percentage (1% = 1°)
settings.north_ccw_info: Set azimuth display: counter-clockwise (north at top, east at left) or clockwise
settings.bucket_list_info: Targets to always include (one per line). These will show if visible, ignoring constraints. Use the 'name' attribute for identification.
settings.bucket_list_title: Personal bucket list - targets that will always be included if visible
settings.done_list_info: Targets to never show (one per line). Use the 'name' attribute for identification.
settings.done_list_title: Targets you've already observed and want to exclude
settings.custom_targets_info: Define custom targets in YAML format. Required fields: name, ra, dec. Optional: description, type, constellation, size, mag.
settings.custom_targets_title: Add your own custom targets not in the standard catalogues
settings.horizon_info: Define horizon anchor points in YAML format. Anchor points should start with azimuth 0 and end with 360 degrees. Feature Horizon must be enabled.
settings.horizon_config_title: Define your custom horizon profile with altitude/azimuth pairs
settings.horizon_profile_info: Define the minimum observable altitude at each azimuth direction to exclude targets blocked by trees, hills, or buildings. Leave empty to use the flat altitude minimum.
settings.horizon_profile_warn_invalid_rows: {count} row(s) with invalid values will be skipped.
settings.horizon_profile_err_duplicate_az: Duplicate azimuth value(s): {list}°. Each azimuth must be unique.
settings.horizon_profile_warn_too_few: At least 2 points are needed for a meaningful horizon profile.
settings.skytonight_reload_info: When new SkyTonight settings are saved, a manual run may be required to see changes on dashboard, or wait for the next scheduled run.
settings.guide_altitude_desc: Targets must reach an altitude between min and max during the observable night. The green band is the valid zone.
settings.guide_airmass_desc: Airmass measures the thickness of atmosphere a light ray crosses. Lower altitude = more atmosphere = worse quality. This constraint overrides min altitude if stricter.
settings.guide_size_desc: Filters deep-sky objects by apparent size. Use to remove objects too small to resolve or too large for your sensor's field of view.
settings.guide_moon_desc: Targets within the exclusion zone around the moon are filtered out. With dynamic mode enabled, the minimum gap automatically scales with moon brightness.
settings.guide_time_desc: A target must be above all constraints for at least this fraction of the night. Lower values include objects visible only briefly near dusk or dawn.
settings.geolocation_unsupported: Geolocation is not supported by your browser.
settings.at_least_one_catalogue: At least one catalogue must be selected
settings.backup_info: Download a ZIP archive of your personal data. The archive includes configuration, user accounts, Astrodex entries and equipment profiles.
settings.restore_warning: Restoring a backup will overwrite the current configuration, users, Astrodex and equipment data with the contents of the archive. This action cannot be undone.
settings.restore_no_file: Please select a backup archive (.zip) to restore.
settings.restore_confirm: Are you sure you want to restore this backup? Current data will be overwritten and cannot be recovered.
settings.log_level_info: The log level controls how much detail is written to the log files. To capture the most detail for troubleshooting, set the level to DEBUG via the LOG_LEVEL environment variable, restart the application, reproduce the issue, then export the logs below.
settings.log_level_env_hint: Set in docker-compose.yml or .env, then restart the container.
settings.log_export_info: Download a ZIP archive of all log files. Share this archive when reporting an issue to help with diagnostics.
settings.notifications_info: Control which events trigger alerts. Notifications appear even when this tab is in the background.
settings.notifications_permission_default: Browser notifications are not yet enabled.
settings.notifications_permission_granted: Browser notifications are enabled. To revoke, open your browser site settings.
settings.notifications_permission_denied: Browser notifications are blocked. Enable them in your browser settings.
settings.notifications_unsupported: Your browser does not support notifications.
settings.notifications_push_inapp_only: In-app only — tab must be open

astrodex.title: Astrodex - Gotta Catch 'Em All!
astrodex.your_collection: Your personal collection of captured celestial objects
astrodex.shared_collection: Shared collection of captured celestial objects (read-only for items from other users)
astrodex.start_adding: Start adding objects to your Astrodex! You can add any celestial object you have observed, along with photos and details. Your collection will grow over time as you capture more of the night sky. Happy stargazing!
astrodex.read_only_user: As read-only user, you can't add new objects.
astrodex.item_already_exists: This object is already in your Astrodex
astrodex.form_notes_placeholder: Add any details about your observation, such as location, equipment used, or special conditions.
astrodex.confirm_delete_photo: Are you sure you want to delete this photo? This action cannot be undone.
astrodex.confirm_delete_item: Are you sure you want to remove this object from your Astrodex? This will also delete all associated photos. This action cannot be undone.
astrodex.failed_to_delete_item: Failed to remove object from Astrodex

skytonight.info_1: SkyTonight calculates the best observation targets for the night based on your location, weather forecast, and internal datasets.
skytonight.info_2: Wait before loading, or check parameters if a calculation failed.
skytonight.no_data_available: Currently no data available for SkyTonight service.
skytonight.calculation_in_progress: Calculation in progress — partial data shown, please wait…
skytonight.skytonight_credit: These data and plots are generated by SkyTonight.
skytonight.sky_map_hint: North at top · centre = zenith · rings = altitude (30° / 60° / 90°) · numbers = rank by AstroScore · arc = night trajectory
skytonight.failed_to_load_log_content: Failed to load SkyTonight log content
skytonight.no_report_available: No reports available for this dataset.
skytonight.plan_clear_required: Clear your previous plan to start a new night plan.
skytonight.report_empty: Report file is empty or not available.
skytonight.telescope_reco_no_result: No recommendation available for this target.
skytonight.bodies_elongation_info: Solar elongation is the angular distance between a solar system body and the Sun as seen from Earth. A larger angle means the body is better separated from the Sun and easier to observe at night.
skytonight.altitude_time_horizon_stale: Custom horizon not shown — re-run SkyTonight calculations to include it.
skytonight.footer_source: Data source: local SkyTonight calculations using your configured catalogues and observing constraints (Astropy/Astroplan engine).
skytonight.target_debug_intro: Search any object to see whether it passes tonight's SkyTonight constraints — and why it may be filtered out.
skytonight.target_debug_not_found: Object not found in any catalogue.
skytonight.target_debug_no_night: No nautical night found for tonight at your configured location.
skytonight.target_debug_visible: This object passes all constraints and would appear in tonight's SkyTonight results.
skytonight.target_debug_filtered: This object is filtered out by one or more constraints.

plan_my_night.section_intro: Build a private timeline for tonight directly from SkyTonight targets.
plan_my_night.failed_to_load: Failed to load Plan My Night
plan_my_night.read_only_message: Read-only users cannot access Plan My Night.
plan_my_night.no_plan_message: No plan exists for this night yet. Add targets from SkyTonight to create your plan.
plan_my_night.previous_plan_message: This plan belongs to the previous night. You can add targets to Astrodex or clear the plan.
plan_my_night.overflow_warning: Planned duration exceeds the night range by {overflow}.
plan_my_night.empty_plan_targets: Your plan exists but has no targets yet.
plan_my_night.confirm_clear_all: Clear all plans for all telescopes?
plan_my_night.target_added: Target added to Plan My Night
plan_my_night.failed_to_add_target: Failed to add target to Plan My Night

users.manage_users: Manage users and their access roles.
users.username_too_short: Username must be at least 3 characters
users.password_change_missing_fields: Current password and new password are required
users.password_too_short: Password must be at least 6 characters
users.password_must_be_different: New password must be different from current password
users.required_username_password_role: Username, password, and role are required
users.required_update_payload: Username, password, or role is required
users.cannot_delete_own_account: You cannot delete your own account
users.confirm_delete_user: Are you sure you want to delete user '{username}'? This action cannot be undone.

metrics.info_title: System performance and resource monitoring. Data refreshes automatically every 5 seconds.
metrics.name: Name:
metrics.no_running_in_container: Not running in container (bare metal or VM)
metrics.cache_jobs_info: Each cache job has its own TTL. The scheduler polls every 5 min but only runs jobs whose TTL has expired.

equipment.eq_combinations_description: Combine telescope, camera, mount, accessories, and filters to analyze setup compatibility and FOV. This setup will be also selectable on Astrodex.
equipment.telescopes_description: Manage your telescope profiles with automatic calculation of focal ratios and effective focal lengths.
equipment.cameras_description: Manage your camera profiles with automatic calculation of sensor diagonal and image scale.
equipment.mounts_description: Manage your mount profiles with automatic calculation of recommended imaging payload.
equipment.filters_description: Manage your filter profiles for LRGB, narrowband, broadband, and other imaging filters.
equipment.accessories_description: Manage your accessory profiles for focusers, field flatteners, filter wheels, and other equipment.
equipment.no_equipment_yet: No equipment combinations created yet. Create one to analyze your setup!
equipment.please_select_telescope_camera: Please select at least a telescope and a camera to calculate FOV
equipment.form_type_accessory_placeholder: e.g., Focuser, Field Flattener, Filter Wheel
equipment.form_no_filters_created: No filters available. Please add filters to create combinations.
equipment.form_no_accessories_created: No accessories available. Please add accessories to create combinations.
equipment.confirm_delete_item: Are you sure you want to delete this item?
equipment.shared_readonly_hint: This equipment is owned by another user (read-only)
equipment.combination_broken_share: Some equipment in this combination is no longer shared
equipment.exposure_calc_no_equipment: Add at least one telescope and one camera to use the exposure calculator.
equipment.exposure_calc_method_note: Sub-exposure is set so sky noise variance equals 5× read noise variance, ensuring sky-limited imaging.
equipment.exposure_calc_invalid_rn: Enter a valid read noise value.
equipment.exposure_calc_help_telescope: Effective focal length and f-ratio are used for the plate scale calculation.
equipment.exposure_calc_help_camera: Pixel size sets the angle each pixel covers. Read noise is auto-filled from your camera profile.
equipment.exposure_calc_help_read_noise: Electrons of noise added per readout. Lower is better. Typically 1–6 e⁻ for modern CMOS.
equipment.exposure_calc_help_qe: Fraction of incoming photons converted to electrons. 60–75% is typical for modern CMOS cameras.
equipment.exposure_calc_help_hours: Total planned session length. The calculator divides it into the recommended sub-exposures.
equipment.exposure_calc_help_bortle: Bortle 1 = pristine dark sky (SQM 22). Bortle 9 = inner-city glow (SQM 17.5). The sky background rate scales with this.

weather.loading_astro_failed: Astronomical weather data temporarily unavailable. Please retry shortly.

events_api.solar_eclipse_description: {eclipse_type} eclipse with {obscuration_percent}% obscuration. Peak at {peak_altitude_deg}° altitude.
events_api.lunar_eclipse_description: {eclipse_type} lunar eclipse with {obscuration_percent}% coverage at peak.
events_api.iss_solar_transit_description: ISS crosses the solar disk from your location. Minimum separation {minimum_separation_arcmin}′, estimated transit window {duration_seconds}s near {sun_altitude_deg}° solar altitude. Certified solar filter required.
events_api.iss_lunar_transit_description: ISS crosses the lunar disk from your location. Minimum separation {minimum_separation_arcmin}′, estimated transit window {duration_seconds}s near {moon_altitude_deg}° lunar altitude. Moon illumination {moon_illumination_pct}%.

planetary.conjunction_description: {planet1} and {planet2} appear very close in the sky
planetary.opposition_description: {planet} is at opposition (best visibility). Optimal for observation.
planetary.elongation_description: {planet} reaches maximum elongation ({elongation_degrees}°). Best viewing time.
planetary.retrograde_description: {planet} appears to move backward for ~{duration_days} days.

solar_system.meteor_shower_description: Meteor shower peak. Up to {zenith_hourly_rate} meteors/hour. Parent body: {parent_body}
solar_system.comet_description: Periodic comet near perihelion. Magnitude ~{magnitude} ({visibility}). Check charts for exact location.
solar_system.asteroid_occultation_title: Asteroid Occultation - See IOTA for Details
solar_system.asteroid_occultation_description: Asteroid passing in front of a star. Precise predictions available at IOTA/IOD. Requires precise timing.

special_phenomena.milky_way_description: Galactic center visible at {gc_altitude}° altitude. Excellent night for wide-field astrophotography.
special_phenomena.spring_equinox_description: First day of spring. Equal day and night length. Sun directly above equator.
special_phenomena.summer_solstice_description: First day of summer. Longest day of the year in Northern Hemisphere.
special_phenomena.autumn_equinox_description: First day of autumn. Equal day and night length. Sun directly above equator.
special_phenomena.winter_solstice_description: First day of winter. Shortest day of the year in Northern Hemisphere.
special_phenomena.zodiacal_light_description: Faint cone of light from interplanetary dust visible during twilight. Best viewed in dark skies.

weather_alerts.critical_wind_conditions: Critical wind conditions for tracking starting at {time}
weather_alerts.conditions_next_6_hours: Conditions affecting astrophotography in the next 6 hours

auth.default_password_warning_alert: You are using the default password.
auth.default_password_warning_request: Please change it in the Users section for security.
auth.session_expired: Your session has expired. Please log in again
auth.remember_me_30_days: Remember me (stay logged in for 30 days)
auth.enter_username_password: Please enter both username and password
auth.network_error_retry: Network error. Please check your connection and try again.

cache.updating_data_parallel_details: Updating astronomical data ({remainingTasks} tasks remaining, {completedTasks}/{totalTasks} completed) {progress}% - {stepName}

pwa.install_prompt_unavailable: Install prompt is not currently available in this browser.
pwa.install_dismissed: Install dismissed. You can install later from the browser menu.
pwa.install_ready: Install MyAstroBoard for faster launch and offline access.
pwa.running_installed: MyAstroBoard is running as an installed app.
pwa.ios_add_to_home: On iOS, use Share -> Add to Home Screen to install this app.
pwa.offline_support_unavailable: Background offline support is unavailable right now.
pwa.offline_message: MyAstroBoard cannot reach the server right now. You can keep viewing cached pages and retry once your connection is back.
pwa.still_offline: Still offline. Please verify your network and try again.

object_info.title: Object Information
object_info.not_found_detail: No data was found for "{{identifier}}" in the SIMBAD database.

notifications.n6_body: Night begins in {minutes} min — time to get ready
